content.text: Add an installation instructions section to the README

result.output:
1	# git-sync
2	
3	`git-sync` mirrors refs from a source remote to a target remote without creating a local checkout. It uses an in-memory `go-git` object store and talks smart HTTP directly:
4	
5	- `info/refs` ref advertisement for source and target
6	- `upload-pack` fetch from source with target tip hashes advertised as `have`
7	- `receive-pack` push to target with explicit ref update commands and a streamed packfile
8	
9	That keeps the target side incremental without fetching target objects into the local process first.
10	
11	## Why This Exists
12	
13	Mirroring Git data between remotes usually means a local mirror clone followed by a mirror push. That's fine for small repos but turns a remote-to-remote operation into a local storage problem at scale, and shell glue around `git fetch` / `git push` tends to skip planning and structured output.
14	
15	`git-sync` fills that gap. It streams source packs directly into target `receive-pack` when it can, plans every action before pushing, and emits typed JSON for automation.
16	
17	For when to use it (and when not), see [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md).
18	
19	## Commands
20	
21	The main commands are:
22	
23	- `git-sync sync`: mirror source refs into the target
24	- `git-sync replicate`: overwrite target refs to match source via relay, and fail rather than materialize locally
25	
26	`sync` automatically bootstraps an empty target, so the same command covers initial seeding and ongoing sync. To preview what would happen without pushing, run `git-sync plan` — it takes the same flags as `sync`, and `--mode replicate` previews a `replicate` run.
27	
28	Additional commands (`bootstrap`, `probe`, `fetch`) and advanced flags are available through `git-sync --help` and the unstable library surface. They are not part of the recommended public surface.
29	
30	## Library API
31	
32	`git-sync` is also a Go library. Use `entire.io/entire/gitsync` for the stable embedding surface (`Probe`, `Plan`, `Sync`, `Replicate`, typed results, auth and HTTP injection). `entire.io/entire/gitsync/unstable` exposes advanced controls (`Bootstrap`, `Fetch`, batching knobs, heap measurement) and is not stable.
33	
34	## Quick Start
35	
36	```bash
37	go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
38	  --source-token "$GITSYNC_SOURCE_TOKEN" \
39	  --target-token "$GITSYNC_TARGET_TOKEN" \
40	  https://github.com/source-org/source-repo.git \
41	  https://github.com/target-org/target-repo.git
42	```
43	
44	## Examples
45	
46	Run a replication that overwrites differing target refs, and fail instead of falling back to local materialization:
47	
48	```bash
49	go run ./cmd/git-sync replicate \
50	  --stats \
51	  https://github.com/source-org/source-repo.git \
52	  https://github.com/target-org/target-repo.git
53	```
54	
55	If `replicate` cannot use relay against the target, it fails and tells you to rerun with `sync`.
56	
57	For very large initial migrations, add `--target-max-pack-bytes` to split the initial pack into multiple smaller batches. The same flag works on `sync`, since `sync` auto-bootstraps on empty targets:
58	
59	```bash
60	go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
61	  --target-max-pack-bytes 536870912 \
62	  --protocol v2 \
63	  -v \
64	  <source-url> \
65	  <target-url>
66	```
67	
68	Add `--measure-memory` to any command to sample elapsed time and Go heap usage:
69	
70	```bash
71	go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
72	  --measure-memory \
73	  --json \
74	  <source-url> \
75	  <target-url>
76	```
77	
78	## Sync Behavior
79	
80	`sync` picks the bootstrap relay path automatically when the target is empty. For non-empty targets, safe fast-forward updates also use a relay path that streams the source pack directly into target `receive-pack` without local materialization. Anything not relay-eligible (force, prune, deletes, tag retargets) falls back to a materialized path bounded by `--materialized-max-objects`.
81	
82	Sync specific branches:
83	
84	```bash
85	go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
86	  --branch main,release \
87	  --source-token "$GITSYNC_SOURCE_TOKEN" \
88	  --target-token "$GITSYNC_TARGET_TOKEN" \
89	  <source-url> \
90	  <target-url>
91	```
92	
93	Map a source branch to a different target branch:
94	
95	```bash
96	go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
97	  --map main:stable \
98	  <source-url> \
99	  <target-url>
100	```
101	
102	Mirror tags and prune managed target refs that disappeared from source:
103	
104	```bash
105	go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
106	  --tags \
107	  --prune \
108	  <source-url> \
109	  <target-url>
110	```
111	
112	Force source-side protocol v2:
113	
114	```bash
115	go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
116	  --protocol v2 \
117	  <source-url> \
118	  <target-url>
119	```
120	
121	## JSON Output
122	
123	Add `--json` to any command to emit machine-readable output instead of the default text format.
124	
125	The JSON interface is stable:
126	
127	- keys use `camelCase`
128	- refs and hashes are serialized as strings, not raw byte arrays
129	- top-level keys include `plans`, `pushed`, `skipped`, `blocked`, `deleted`, `dryRun`, `protocol`, and `stats`, plus `relay`, `relayMode`, `relayReason`, `batching`, `batchCount`, `plannedBatchCount`, and `tempRefs`
130	- each item in `plans` includes stable string fields such as `branch`, `sourceRef`, `targetRef`, `sourceHash`, `targetHash`, `kind`, `action`, and `reason`
131	
132	## Auth
133	
134	For GitHub and similar providers, use basic auth with a token as the password.
135	
136	Auth is resolved in this order:
137	
138	- explicit CLI flags
139	- `GITSYNC_*` environment variables
140	- local `git credential fill` helper lookup for `http` and `https` remotes
141	- anonymous access
142	
143	Relevant variables:
144	
145	- `GITSYNC_SOURCE_TOKEN`
146	- `GITSYNC_TARGET_TOKEN`
147	- `GITSYNC_SOURCE_USERNAME` default: `git`
148	- `GITSYNC_TARGET_USERNAME` default: `git`
149	
150	Bearer auth is also available:
151	
152	- `GITSYNC_SOURCE_BEARER_TOKEN`
153	- `GITSYNC_TARGET_BEARER_TOKEN`
154	
155	That means local testing against a dummy GitHub repo can reuse your regular Git credential helper setup without passing tokens on every command.
156	
157	## Protocol Notes
158	
159	- Source-side discovery and fetch can use protocol v2 when supported. Push stays on the existing v1 `receive-pack` path. `--protocol auto` tries v2 first and falls back to v1. `--protocol v2` requires the source to negotiate v2.
160	- Source fetch advertises current target tip hashes as `have`, so reruns download less when source and target already share history.
161	- Branches are updated only when the target tip is an ancestor of the source tip, unless `--force` is set. Tags are immutable by default. Retargeting an existing tag requires `--force`. With `--prune`, managed target refs that are absent on source are deleted.
162	- If `sync` finds blocked refs, it exits non-zero before pushing anything.
163	- `--stats` adds per-service request, byte, want, have, and command counters to the output.
164	
165	For the deeper protocol-level walkthrough (smart HTTP, pkt-line, capability negotiation, sideband stripping, relay framing), see [docs/protocol.md](docs/protocol.md).
166	
167	## Testing
168	
169	Default suite:
170	
171	```bash
172	env GOCACHE=/tmp/go-build go test ./...
173	```
174	
175	Extended and environment-specific test instructions are in [docs/testing.md](docs/testing.md).
176	
177	## Documentation
178	
179	- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md) — product rationale, package layout, operation modes vs transfer modes, memory model
180	- [docs/protocol.md](docs/protocol.md) — smart HTTP, pkt-line, capability negotiation, sideband, relay framing
181	- [docs/testing.md](docs/testing.md) — test suites and integration coverage
182	
183	## Contributing
184	
185	See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md), [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md), and [CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).
186

1	# Contributing to git-sync
2	
3	Thank you for your interest in contributing to Entire! We welcome contributions from everyone.
4	
5	Please read our [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) before participating.
6	
7	> **New to Entire?** See the [README](README.md) for setup and usage documentation.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## Before You Code: Discuss First
12	
13	The fastest way to get a contribution merged is to align with maintainers before writing code. Please **open an issue first** using our [issue templates](https://github.com/entireio/gitsync/issues/new/choose) and wait for maintainer feedback before starting implementation.
14	
15	### Contribution Workflow
16	
17	1. **Open an issue** describing the problem or feature
18	2. **Wait for maintainer feedback** -- we may have relevant context or plans
19	3. **Get approval** before starting implementation
20	4. **Submit your PR** referencing the approved issue
21	5. **Address all feedback** including automated Copilot comments
22	6. **Maintainer review and merge**
23	
24	---
25	
26	## First-Time Contributors
27	
28	New to the project? Welcome! Here's how to get started:
29	
30	### Good First Issues
31	
32	We recommend starting with:
33	- **Documentation improvements** - Fix typos, clarify explanations, add examples
34	- **Test contributions** - Add test cases, improve coverage
35	- **Small bug fixes** - Issues labeled `good-first-issue`
36	
37	---
38	
39	## Submitting Issues
40	
41	All feature requests, bug reports, and general issues should be submitted through [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/entireio/gitsync/issues). Please search for existing issues before opening a new one.
42	
43	For security-related issues, see the Security section below.
44	
45	---
46	
47	## Security
48	
49	If you discover a security vulnerability, **do not report it through GitHub Issues**. Instead, please follow the instructions in our [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md) file for responsible disclosure. All security reports are kept confidential as described in SECURITY.md.
50	
51	---
52	
53	## Contributions & Communication
54	
55	Contributions and communications are expected to occur through:
56	
57	- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/entireio/gitsync/issues) - Bug reports and feature requests
58	- [Discord](https://discord.gg/jZJs3Tue4S) - Questions, general conversation, and real-time support
59	
60	Please represent the project and community respectfully in all public and private interactions.
61	
62	
63	## How to Contribute
64	
65	
66	There are many ways to contribute:
67	
68	- **Feature requests** - Open a [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/entireio/gitsync/issues) to discuss your idea
69	- **Bug reports** - Report issues via [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/entireio/gitsync/issues) (see [Reporting Bugs](#reporting-bugs))
70	- **Code contributions** - Fix bugs, add features, improve tests
71	- **Documentation** - Improve guides, fix typos, add examples
72	- **Community** - Help others, answer questions, share knowledge
73	
74	
75	## Reporting Bugs
76	
77	Good bug reports help us fix issues quickly. When reporting a bug, please include:
78	
79	### Required Information
80	
81	1. **git-sync commit** - `git rev-parse HEAD` of the build you used (or release tag/version if applicable)
82	2. **Operating system**
83	3. **Go version** - run `go version`
84	
85	### What to Include
86	
87	Please answer these questions in your bug report:
88	
89	1. **What did you do?** - Include the exact commands you ran
90	2. **What did you expect to happen?**
91	3. **What actually happened?** - Include the full error message or unexpected output
92	4. **Can you reproduce it?** - Does it happen every time or intermittently?
93	5. **Any additional context?** - Logs, screenshots, or related issues
94	
95	---
96	
97	## Local Setup
98	
99	### Prerequisites
100	
101	- **mise** - Task runner and version manager. Install with `curl https://mise.run | sh`
102	
103	### Clone and Install
104	
105	```bash
106	git clone https://github.com/entireio/gitsync.git
107	cd gitsync
108	
109	# Trust the mise configuration (required on first setup)
110	mise trust
111	
112	# Install dependencies (mise will install the correct Go version)
113	mise install
114	
115	# Download Go modules
116	go mod download
117	
118	# Build the CLI
119	mise run build
120	
121	# Verify setup by running tests
122	mise run test
123	```
124	
125	> See [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md) for the architecture and package layout.
126	
127	---
128	
129	## Making Changes
130	
131	1. **Create a branch** for your changes:
132	   ```bash
133	   git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
134	   ```
135	
136	2. **Make your changes** - follow the [Code Style](#code-style) guidelines
137	
138	3. **Test your changes** - see [Testing](#testing)
139	
140	4. **Commit** with clear, descriptive messages:
141	   ```bash
142	   git commit -m "Add feature: description of what you added"
143	   ```
144	
145	---
146	
147	## Code Style
148	
149	Follow standard Go idioms and conventions.
150	
151	### Key Points
152	
153	- **Error handling**: Handle all errors explicitly - don't leave them unchecked
154	- **Formatting**: Code must pass `gofmt` (run `mise run fmt`)
155	- **Linting**: Code must pass `golangci-lint` (run `mise run lint`)
156	- **Naming**: Use meaningful, descriptive names following Go conventions
157	- **Public API**: `entire.io/entire/gitsync` is the stable embedding surface. Additions there should be reviewed carefully. `entire.io/entire/gitsync/unstable` is for advanced controls and may change.
158	
159	---
160	
161	## Testing
162	
163	> See [docs/testing.md](docs/testing.md) for the full set of test suites and integration coverage.
164	
165	```bash
166	# Default suite - always run before committing
167	mise run test
168	
169	# With race detection
170	mise run test:ci
171	
172	# Optional end-to-end against the system git-http-backend
173	mise run test:git-http-backend
174	
175	# Optional live linux bootstrap smokes
176	mise run test:linux-smoke
177	mise run test:linux-smoke:batched
178	```
179	
180	---
181	
182	## Submitting a Pull Request
183	
184	### Before You Submit
185	
186	- **Related issue exists and is approved** -- Your PR references an issue where a maintainer has acknowledged the approach. (Exceptions: documentation fixes, typo corrections, and `good-first-issue` items.)
187	- **Linting passes** -- Run `mise run lint` (includes golangci-lint, gofmt, gomod, shellcheck)
188	- **Tests pass** -- Run `mise run test` to verify your changes
189	- **Tests included** -- New Go code and functionality should have accompanying tests
190	- **Entire checkpoint trailers included** -- See [Using Entire While Contributing](#using-entire-while-contributing) below
191	
192	PRs that skip these steps are likely to be closed without merge.
193	
194	### Submitting
195	
196	1. **Push** your branch to your fork
197	2. **Open a PR** against the `main` branch
198	3. **Describe your changes** -- Link the related issue, summarize what changed and what testing you did
199	4. **Address Copilot feedback** -- See [Responding to Automated Review](#responding-to-automated-review)
200	5. **Wait for maintainer review**
201	
202	---
203	
204	## Responding to Automated Review
205	
206	Copilot agent reviews every PR and provides feedback on code quality, potential bugs, and project conventions.
207	
208	**Read and respond to every Copilot comment.** PRs with unaddressed Copilot feedback will not move to maintainer review.
209	
210	- **Fixed** -- Push a commit addressing the issue.
211	- **Disagree** -- Reply explaining your reasoning. The Copilot isn't always right.
212	- **Question** -- Ask for clarification. We're happy to help.
213	
214	Addressing Copilot feedback upfront is the fastest path to maintainer review.
215	
216	---
217	
218	## Using Entire While Contributing
219	
220	We use Entire on Entire. When contributing, install the Entire CLI and let it capture your coding sessions -- this gives us valuable dogfooding data and helps improve the tool.
221	
222	### Setup
223	
224	Install the latest version of the Entire CLI (see [installation docs](https://docs.entire.io/cli/installation)) and verify with `entire version`. Entire is already configured in this repository, so there's no need to run `entire enable`.
225	
226	### Checkpoint Trailers
227	
228	All commits should include `Entire-Checkpoint` trailers from your sessions. These are added automatically by the `prepare-commit-msg` hook when Entire is enabled. The trailers link your commits to session metadata on the `entire/checkpoints/v1` branch.
229	
230	### Sessions Branch
231	
232	When you push your PR branch, Entire can automatically push the `entire/checkpoints/v1` branch alongside it (if `push_sessions` is enabled in your settings). Include this in your PR so maintainers can review the session context behind your changes.
233	
234	---
235	
236	## Troubleshooting
237	
238	### Common Setup Issues
239	
240	**`go mod download` fails with timeout**
241	```bash
242	# Try using direct mode
243	GOPROXY=direct go mod download
244	```
245	
246	**`mise install` fails**
247	```bash
248	# Ensure mise is properly installed
249	curl https://mise.run | sh
250	
251	# Reload your shell
252	source ~/.zshrc  # or ~/.bashrc
253	```
254	
255	**Binary not updating after rebuild**
256	```bash
257	# Check which binary is being used
258	which git-sync
259	type -a git-sync
260	
261	# You may have multiple installations - update the correct path
262	```
263	
264	---
265	
266	## Community
267	
268	Join the Entire community:
269	
270	- **Discord** - [Join our server][discord] for discussions and support
271	
272	[discord]: https://discord.gg/jZJs3Tue4S
273	
274	---
275	
276	## Additional Resources
277	
278	- [README](README.md) - Setup and usage documentation
279	- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md) - Architecture and package layout
280	- [docs/testing.md](docs/testing.md) - Test suites and integration coverage
281	- [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) - Community guidelines
282	- [Security Policy](SECURITY.md) - Reporting security vulnerabilities
283	
284	---
285	
286	Thank you for contributing!
287

2	# Please also keep the version aligned in the go.mod file
3	go = { version = '1.26.2', postinstall = "go install github.com/go-delve/delve/cmd/dlv@latest && go install gotest.tools/gotestsum@latest" }
4	golangci-lint = '2.11.4'
5	shellcheck = 'latest'
6	
7	[tasks.fmt]
8	description = "Run gofmt"
9	run = "gofmt -s -w ."
10	
11	[tasks.test]
12	description = "Run tests"
13	env = { GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT = "0" }
14	run = "go test ./..."
15	
16	[tasks."test:ci"]
17	description = "Run all tests with race detection"
18	env = { GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT = "0" }
19	run = "go test -race ./..."
20	
21	[tasks."test:git-http-backend"]
22	description = "Run optional git-http-backend integration tests"
23	run = "GITSYNC_E2E_GIT_HTTP_BACKEND=1 go test ./internal/syncer -run 'TestRun_GitHTTPBackendSync|TestBootstrap_GitHTTPBackendSync' -v"
24	
25	[tasks."test:linux-smoke"]
26	description = "Run optional live linux bootstrap smoke test"
27	run = "GITSYNC_E2E_LIVE_LINUX=1 go test ./internal/syncer -run TestBootstrap_LiveLinuxSource -timeout 60m -v"
28	
29	[tasks."test:linux-smoke:batched"]
30	description = "Run optional live linux batched bootstrap smoke test"
31	run = "GITSYNC_E2E_LIVE_LINUX=1 go test ./internal/syncer -run TestBootstrap_LiveLinuxSourceBatched -timeout 60m -v"
32	
33	[tasks."test:entire-local-smoke"]
34	description = "Run optional smoke syncing a public repo into a running Entire local instance"
35	run = "GITSYNC_E2E_ENTIRE=1 go test ./internal/syncer -run TestRun_EntireLocalPublicRepoSmoke -timeout 30m -v"
36	
37	[tasks."test:entire-local-smoke:linux"]
38	description = "Run optional batched smoke syncing the Linux repo into a running Entire local instance"
39	run = "GITSYNC_E2E_ENTIRE=1 GITSYNC_E2E_ENTIRE_SOURCE_URL=https://github.com/torvalds/linux.git GITSYNC_E2E_ENTIRE_BRANCH=master GITSYNC_E2E_ENTIRE_PROTOCOL=v2 GITSYNC_E2E_ENTIRE_BATCH_MAX_PACK_BYTES=536870912 go test ./internal/syncer -run TestRun_EntireLocalPublicRepoSmoke -timeout 60m -v"
40	
41	[tasks."test:entire-local-smoke:linux:single"]
42	description = "Run optional single-pack smoke syncing the Linux repo into a running Entire local instance"
43	run = "GITSYNC_E2E_ENTIRE=1 GITSYNC_E2E_ENTIRE_SOURCE_URL=https://github.com/torvalds/linux.git GITSYNC_E2E_ENTIRE_BRANCH=master go test ./internal/syncer -run TestRun_EntireLocalPublicRepoSmoke -timeout 60m -v"
44	
45	[tasks.build]
46	description = "Build git-sync"
47	run = "go build ./cmd/git-sync"

content.text: I'll add an Installation section before Quick Start.

input.new_string:
## Installation

Requires Go 1.26 or newer.

Install the latest release with `go install`:

```bash
go install entire.io/entire/gitsync/cmd/git-sync@latest
```

This drops a `git-sync` binary into `$(go env GOPATH)/bin`. Make sure that directory is on your `PATH`.

Or build from source:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/entireio/gitsync.git
cd gitsync
go build -o git-sync ./cmd/git-sync
```

The examples below use `go run ./cmd/git-sync` so they work from a source checkout. If you installed the binary, swap that for `git-sync`.

## Quick Start

```bash
go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
  --source-token "$GITSYNC_SOURCE_TOKEN" \
  --target-token "$GITSYNC_TARGET_TOKEN" \
  https://github.com/source-org/source-repo.git \
  https://github.com/target-org/target-repo.git
```

content.text: Added an Installation section to README.md:8 covering `go install` and build-from-source, with a note that the examples use `go run ./cmd/git-sync` and how to swap in the installed binary.
Just swap the examples below to use git-sync

input.old_string:
The examples below use `go run ./cmd/git-sync` so they work from a source checkout. If you installed the binary, swap that for `git-sync`.

## Quick Start

```bash
go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
  --source-token "$GITSYNC_SOURCE_TOKEN" \
  --target-token "$GITSYNC_TARGET_TOKEN" \
  https://github.com/source-org/source-repo.git \
  https://github.com/target-org/target-repo.git
```

content.text: Swapped all `go run ./cmd/git-sync` invocations in the README for `git-sync` and dropped the now-unneeded swap note from the Installation section.

## Context

- Current git status: On branch main
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.

Changes not staged for commit:
  (use "git add <file>..." to update what will be committed)
  (use "git restore <file>..." to discard changes in working directory)
	modified:   README.md

no changes added to commit (use "git add" and/or "git commit -a")
- Current git diff (staged and unstaged changes): diff --git c/README.md w/README.md
index 1196da1..e179c55 100644
--- c/README.md
+++ w/README.md
@@ -31,10 +31,30 @@ Additional commands (`bootstrap`, `probe`, `fetch`) and advanced flags are avail
 
 `git-sync` is also a Go library. Use `entire.io/entire/gitsync` for the stable embedding surface (`Probe`, `Plan`, `Sync`, `Replicate`, typed results, auth and HTTP injection). `entire.io/entire/gitsync/unstable` exposes advanced controls (`Bootstrap`, `Fetch`, batching knobs, heap measurement) and is not stable.
 
+## Installation
+
+Requires Go 1.26 or newer.
+
+Install the latest release with `go install`:
+
+```bash
+go install entire.io/entire/gitsync/cmd/git-sync@latest
+```
+
+This drops a `git-sync` binary into `$(go env GOPATH)/bin`. Make sure that directory is on your `PATH`.
+
+Or build from source:
+
+```bash
+git clone https://github.com/entireio/gitsync.git
+cd gitsync
+go build -o git-sync ./cmd/git-sync
+```
+
 ## Quick Start
 
 ```bash
-go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
+git-sync sync \
   --source-token "$GITSYNC_SOURCE_TOKEN" \
   --target-token "$GITSYNC_TARGET_TOKEN" \
   https://github.com/source-org/source-repo.git \
@@ -46,7 +66,7 @@ go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
 Run a replication that overwrites differing target refs, and fail instead of falling back to local materialization:
 
 ```bash
-go run ./cmd/git-sync replicate \
+git-sync replicate \
   --stats \
   https://github.com/source-org/source-repo.git \
   https://github.com/target-org/target-repo.git
@@ -57,7 +77,7 @@ If `replicate` cannot use relay against the target, it fails and tells you to re
 For very large initial migrations, add `--target-max-pack-bytes` to split the initial pack into multiple smaller batches. The same flag works on `sync`, since `sync` auto-bootstraps on empty targets:
 
 ```bash
-go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
+git-sync sync \
   --target-max-pack-bytes 536870912 \
   --protocol v2 \
   -v \
@@ -68,7 +88,7 @@ go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
 Add `--measure-memory` to any command to sample elapsed time and Go heap usage:
 
 ```bash
-go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
+git-sync sync \
   --measure-memory \
   --json \
   <source-url> \
@@ -82,7 +102,7 @@ go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
 Sync specific branches:
 
 ```bash
-go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
+git-sync sync \
   --branch main,release \
   --source-token "$GITSYNC_SOURCE_TOKEN" \
   --target-token "$GITSYNC_TARGET_TOKEN" \
@@ -93,7 +113,7 @@ go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
 Map a source branch to a different target branch:
 
 ```bash
-go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
+git-sync sync \
   --map main:stable \
   <source-url> \
   <target-url>
@@ -102,7 +122,7 @@ go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
 Mirror tags and prune managed target refs that disappeared from source:
 
 ```bash
-go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
+git-sync sync \
   --tags \
   --prune \
   <source-url> \
@@ -112,7 +132,7 @@ go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
 Force source-side protocol v2:
 
 ```bash
-go run ./cmd/git-sync sync \
+git-sync sync \
   --protocol v2 \
   <source-url> \
   <target-url>
- Current branch: main

## Your task

Based on the above changes:

1. Create a new branch if on main
2. Create a single commit with an appropriate message
3. Push the branch to origin
4. Create a pull request using `gh pr create`
5. You have the capability to call multiple tools in a single response. You MUST do all of the above in a single message. Do not use any other tools or do anything else. Do not send any other text or messages besides these tool calls.


ARGUMENTS: this fixes issue #20
